name: ct-okf
description: Author and query Open Knowledge Format (OKF v0.1) bundles — directory trees of Markdown concepts whose leading YAML frontmatter carries a required `type` plus optional title/description/resource/tags/timestamp, alongside reserved index.md / log.md files and Markdown cross-links. Choose exactly one verb (default --validate). Read-only verbs: --validate (conformance verdict over the bundle: every non-reserved .md must have parseable frontmatter with a non-empty type; counts violations, default expect none, --strict also fails on broken bundle-relative links); --list (concepts with metadata, filter by --type and --tag); --show PATH (one concept's frontmatter); --links (broken bundle cross-link verdict). Authoring verbs (these write files): --new PATH (scaffold a concept, requires --type, refuses to overwrite); --init (scaffold a bundle-root index.md); --index ((re)generate index.md for --base from concepts' frontmatter); --log MESSAGE (prepend a dated entry to log.md, labelled by --log-kind); --set FIELD=VALUE (set/update a scalar frontmatter field on --file). Targeting uses the suite's walker vocabulary (--base, --name, --hidden, --follow, --no-ignore). Framed verdict on the check verbs: --question banner, --expect over the violation count (any|none|N|=N|+N|-N, default none), --emit/--emit-stderr templates with {RESULT} {QUESTION} {COUNT} {TOTAL} {BASE} {MATCHES}; exit 0 SUCCESS, 1 ERROR, 2 usage/runtime error. Because the authoring verbs write, ct-okf is not on the read-only allowlist (read-only OKF composition is provided by the OKF-aware ct-search/ct-tree/ct-view/ct-outline and the `okf` built-in check). Invoke as `ct okf ...` or `ct-okf ...`.

base.description: Bundle root (or single concept root) to operate on. Default '.'.

name.description: Limit selection to files whose name matches; '|'-separated alternatives, each substring->glob->regex promoted and anchored.

hidden.description: Include dot-entries (names starting with '.'). Default: skipped.

no-ignore.description: Walk gitignored / .ignore files too (the .git directory is always skipped); by default the walk skips what git would.

validate.description: Check the bundle for OKF conformance and report a verdict (the default verb).

list.description: List the bundle's concepts with their metadata; filter with --type / --tag.

show.description: Show one concept's frontmatter (give the concept path).

links.description: Report the bundle's broken bundle-relative cross-links as a verdict.

new.description: Scaffold a new concept at PATH (requires --type); refuses to overwrite.

init.description: Scaffold a bundle-root index.md (declaring okf_version) if absent.

index.description: (Re)generate index.md for --base from the concepts' frontmatter.

log.description: Prepend a dated entry to the bundle's log.md (label it with --log-kind).

set.description: Set or update a scalar frontmatter field on the --file concept: FIELD=VALUE.

script.description: Run a .ctb batch of new/set/log/index/init items atomically under prepare/confirm/write: the whole script is simulated in memory (cascading — a later index/set sees earlier ops' writes), and nothing is written unless every op succeeds. One failing op (e.g. a clobbering new) means zero writes.

dry-run.description: With --script: simulate and print the plan, but write nothing.

fence.description: With --script: the directive prefix for script lines. Default '#%'; change it for payloads containing '#%' at line start.

type.description: The concept `type` for --new; also filters --list to this exact type.

title.description: With --new: the concept title (defaults to the file stem).

tag.description: With --new: tags (comma-joined or repeated); also filters --list to concepts carrying all given tags.

log-kind.description: With --log: the entry label (e.g. Update, Creation). Default: Update.

file.description: With --set: the concept file to edit.

strict.description: With --validate: also treat broken bundle-relative links as violations.

question.description: Question this check answers, framing it as a test; printed as a '== ... ==' banner unless quiet.

expect.description: Verdict expectation over the violation count; default 'none'. One of: any (>=1), none (==0), N (>=N), =N (==N), +N (>N), -N (<N).

emit.description: Template written to stdout after a check (alias: emit-stdout). Tokens: {RESULT} {QUESTION} {COUNT} {TOTAL} {BASE} {MATCHES}.

emit-stderr.description: Template written to stderr after a check. Same tokens as emit.

quiet.description: Suppress informational output; report via exit status (and --emit, which still fires).

json.description: Emit a structured JSON result instead of text; the object's shape depends on the verb (tool, verb, verdict, and the verb's payload).

timeout.description: Abort with exit 2 (and a one-line message) if the run exceeds SECS seconds (fractional allowed).

heartbeat.description: Print a liveness pulse every SECS seconds (fractional allowed) while the run is in progress.

heartbeat-emit.description: Heartbeat line template. Tokens: {ELAPSED} (whole seconds so far) {TOOL}. Default: "[{ELAPSED}s]".

heartbeat-to.description: Stream heartbeat pulses are written to. Default: stderr.
